title: Unidentified female Saarai-kaar

A '''female [[Saarai-kaar]]''' served as the leader of the [[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]] following the death of [[Nikkos Tyris]].

==Biography==
When Tyris and her husband were killed by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Nejaa Halcyon]] and [[Ylenic It'kla]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> she developed a hatred of the Jedi, whom she believed were responsible for murdering her husband and Master (unaware that Tyris was a [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|Dark Acolyte]] of [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]). She assumed the leadership of the Jensaarai and kept them hidden from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] on [[Susevfi]]. However, her son approached [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], offering to become a [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi hunter]]. Vader killed him on sight, and the Saarai-kaar further hid her people.

She trained [[Mei (Dark Jedi)|Mei]], the daughter of [[Sukarr]], and her lover [[Fomadu]] who would seek revenge on [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi "Desertwind"]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] for the murder of her father, some time after the Proclamation of the Empire.

[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Leonia Tavira]], however, later found the Jensaarai, and forced them to help her [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] fleet. The Saarai-kaar discovered [[Mirax Terrik Horn]] among Leonia's pirate crew, and kept her in a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-induced hibernation. When [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], Nejaa Halcyon's grandson, [[Battle of Susevfi|arrived]] on Susevfi with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Elegos A'Kla]], Ylenic's nephew, they fought the Saari-kaar, defeating her. After the battle, Elegos shared the [[memnis]] of Tyris and Halcyon's deaths with the Saarai-kaar, and she was able to forgive the Jedi. She also discussed the possibility of allowing some of the Jensaarai and herself to join Luke Skywalker's [[Jedi Praxeum]].
